methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Asylon Robotics is a Series B-stage autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $40M to date. The broader comp universe consists of eight private peers in this sector, including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Archer Aviation. No direct public comparables exist in the narrow peer set.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, as the peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Drones & Autonomous cohort—only one same-stage peer was identified, below the three-datapoint threshold required for statistical reliability. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $40M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $76M.

This single-method triangulation produces a range of $65M to $87M, with a median point of $76M. The width of this range—approximately ±15% around the median—reflects the inherent uncertainty in applying a historical multiplier to a single anchor point rather than a consensus peer valuation. Key risks to this range include refinement of the $40M proxy as more granular funding tranche data becomes available, and material shifts in comparable company fundraising activity that would inform stage-progression calibration. The analysis would benefit from expansion of the peer set or emergence of recent comparable transactions in the autonomous systems space.
